sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Market | Facilities Support Services |
| NAICS | 561210 |
| Job | Help FM operators detect unauthorized emergency exit door activations and produce audit-ready event logs across client building portfolios — without wired infrastructure or on-site inspection rounds |
| Surviving market | Yes |
| Constraints in play | C3 (range), C4 (battery), C6 (protocol lock-in), C7 (price), C8 (GDPR) |
| Market needs (Step 06) | 18 |
| Product-related needs injected (Step 10) | 4 (PR1–PR4) |
| Total scored entries | **22** |

---

sections.title: Incumbent Recalibration Basis
sections.content:
Satisfaction scores recalibrated against dominant FM alternatives. Source: FIT file (Alt references) and market discovery incumbents.

| Alternative | Share | Key Strengths | Key Weaknesses | Recalibration effect |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Alt A — Wired alarm panels (hardwired) | ~45% | Reliable continuous detection, no battery | High retrofit cost, electrician required, no portfolio dashboard | ↓ Sat on N02, N17 (cost); ↑ Sat on N07 (power independence via mains) |
| Alt C — Mechanical-only door alarms | ~20% | Low cost, no power required, fail-safe | No remote monitoring, no event log, alarm local only | ↓ Sat on N06, N08, N13, N14, N15 (zero monitoring capability) |
| Alt D — Manual patrol rounds + paper logs | ~25% | Low upfront cost | Hours-to-detect latency, manual log assembly, no real-time data | ↓ Sat on N06, N08, N13, N14 (systematic latency and gaps) |

---

sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Market | Facilities Support Services |
| NAICS | 561210 |
| Total needs | 22 |
| **Underserved** (is_underserved=true) | **13** |
| Overserved (is_overserved=true) | **0** |
| **Avg opportunity score** | **11.98** |

**Top 5 opportunities (sorted by score desc):**

| Rank | Score | Need | Statement |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **16.00** | N04 | Minimize door guardians that pass commissioning tests but fail under real operating conditions |
| 2 | **16.00** | N08 | Minimize unauthorized activations going undetected between FM inspection visits |
| 3 | **16.00** | N14 | Minimize event log gaps that cause FM clients to fail unannounced fire safety inspections |
| 4 | **15.00** | N02 | Minimize conduit wiring and electrical installation cost per door in retrofit buildings |
| 5 | **15.00** | N13 | Minimize FM labor-hours to compile audit-ready inspection certificates per building |

**Cross-check (Step 09 Feature-Fit):** FIT avg = 6.53 | ODI avg = 11.98. No inconsistency flag. (Flags: ODI > 12 + FIT < 4 = No; ODI < 8 + FIT > 7 = No.)

---

sections.title: Key Observations
sections.content:
- **13/22 needs underserved** — FM market is chronically underserved. Mechanical-only alarms (Alt C) and manual rounds (Alt D) leave detection, logging, and documentation jobs entirely unmet.
- **Documentation cluster dominates** — N04, N08, N14 (all 16.0) and N13 (15.0) are all about evidence continuity. Incumbents produce no automated audit trail — their failure mode is systematic.
- **N07 is not underserved despite Imp=10** — Mechanical alarm incumbents (Alt C) already satisfy power-independence (Sat=7.0). The Türwächter meets a bar that's already met. No differentiation here.
- **Product-related needs add friction, not opportunity** — PR2 (12.0, battery) and PR3 (11.5, BACnet/KNX rework) are EasyWave architecture liabilities that would disappear with a LoRaWAN or BACnet-native device. They are cost of technology, not market signal.
- **Installation cost (N02=15) is the primary switching barrier** — Alt A's 45% share persists because switching to IoT triggers rewiring cost. The Türwächter's retrofit capability directly addresses this.

---
